release_notes: Fixes a Windows Store autostart bug that produced a 'The specified path does not exist' popup at every login after a Store update. Pre-1.0.33 builds wrote a Startup-folder .bat that hard-coded sys.executable, which on a Store install resolves to C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\FieldcrestDental.ChairsideReadyAlert_<version>_<arch>__<hash>\ChairsideReadyAlert.exe. The Store invalidates that directory on every upgrade, so the .bat permanently pointed at a deleted package directory and the app could not self-heal because _sync_autostart_state only checked file existence, never the path inside. From 1.0.33, autostart on Store builds is owned by an AppxManifest windows.startupTask extension — the AppX runtime re-points the executable to the currently installed package on every update, so no version-scoped path is ever cached. Users manage it in Settings -> Apps -> Startup. The legacy .bat is deleted on the first 1.0.33 launch (via _migrate_legacy_startup_bat) so already-affected users stop seeing the popup. The in-app 'Start automatically at login' checkbox on Store builds is replaced with a 'Manage startup apps (Windows Settings)…' command that opens ms-settings:startupapps. macOS (LaunchAgent plist) and the GitHub-distributed (non-frozen) Windows install (Startup-folder .bat invoking pythonw.exe) are unaffected; their autostart paths are stable across updates.
